message:
fix(acfs): resolve target user/home from state-file path and validate before dispatch

Continuation of the state-file hardening pass started in
6264de17 (drop user-controlled ACFS_BIN_DIR fallback) and
b4ee02fd (drop state files that do not match the requested
TARGET_HOME). The remaining gap: when multiple state files exist
(installed per-user state, system state in /etc, stale siblings
from earlier runs), the dispatcher and status tooling could still
pick a state file whose declared target_home pointed at a different
user than the one actually described by the state file's own path.
That let an attacker with write access to a stale state file steer
the runtime context at another user.

Close the gap by deriving the target home from the state file's
canonical path first, then cross-checking it against /etc/passwd
(getent preferred) and only accepting a target_user whose resolved
home matches the state_home derived from the path. When the path
cannot be trusted (non-/.acfs/state.json layout), fall back to the
in-file target_home, still sanitized as an absolute non-root path.

Scripts touched
---------------
- scripts/acfs-global:
  * new state_file_path_user_home() - canonical path-only
    derivation, sanitized.
  * state_file_matches_target_home() now prefers the path-derived
    home and only falls back to the in-file home when the path is
    ambiguous.
  * home_from_acfs_bin() renamed to acfs_home_from_acfs_bin() and
    generalized to return the .acfs directory for both
    ~/.local/bin/acfs and ~/.acfs/bin/acfs layouts, so downstream
    callers stop having to reconstruct the path.
  * new validated_target_user_for_dispatch() - requires that
    resolve_home_for_user(candidate_user) matches the expected
    home before honoring the candidate.
- scripts/acfs-update: mirrors the acfs-global changes so both
  entry points agree on the resolution rules.
- scripts/lib/info.sh:
  * new info_state_file_path_target_home(),
    info_read_user_for_home(), and info_resolve_target_user().
  * info layer now prefers getent, falls back to /etc/passwd, and
    only considers the state file's in-file user when the resolved
    home agrees.
- scripts/lib/status.sh: threads the new resolver through
  _status_prepare_context so `acfs status` reports the per-user
  install state even when a stale system state file is present.
- scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh: adapts the smoke path so it exercises
  the same resolution logic the real dispatcher now runs.

Tests
-----
- tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh: add
  test_status_prefers_resolved_install_state_over_stale_system_state_for_target_context
  with a freshly installed layout plus a stale system state file
  pointing at a different user, and asserts _status_prepare_context
  resolves TARGET_USER/TARGET_HOME/state path to the per-user
  install.

No behavior change for single-user hosts with one state file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+    local state_file="${1:-}"

+
+    [[ "$state_file" == */.acfs/state.json ]] || return 1
+    data_home="${state_file%/state.json}"

+info_read_user_for_home() {
+    local user_home="${1:-}"
+    local candidate_user=""
+    local candidate_home=""
+    local current_user=""
+    local current_home=""
+    local passwd_line=""
+    local passwd_home=""
+    local state_file=""
+
+    user_home="$(info_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$user_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$user_home" ]] || return 1
+
+    if command -v getent &>/dev/null; then
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            passwd_home="$(info_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$passwd_line" | cut -d: -f6)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+            [[ "$passwd_home" == "$user_home" ]] || continue
+            candidate_user="${passwd_line%%:*}"
+            if [[ "$candidate_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
+                printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < <(getent passwd 2>/dev/null || true)
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -r /etc/passwd ]]; then
+        while IFS= read -r passwd_line; do
+            passwd_home="$(info_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$(printf '%s\n' "$passwd_line" | cut -d: -f6)" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+            [[ "$passwd_home" == "$user_home" ]] || continue
+            candidate_user="${passwd_line%%:*}"
+            if [[ "$candidate_user" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]; then
+                printf '%s\n' "$candidate_user"
+                return 0
+            fi
+        done < /etc/passwd
+    fi
+
+    current_user="$(id -un 2>/dev/null || whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    current_home="${_INFO_CURRENT_HOME:-}"
+    if [[ -z "$current_home" ]]; then
+        current_home="$(info_sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "${HOME:-}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+    if [[ -n "$current_user" ]] && [[ -n "$current_home" ]] && [[ "$user_home" == "$current_home" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$current_user"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$user_home" == "/root" ]]; then
+        printf 'root\n'
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    state_file="$user_home/.acfs/state.json"
